GET /radar/bots/crawlers/summary/{dimens.description: Retrieves an aggregated summary of HTTP requests from crawlers, grouped by the specified dimension.

parameters.description: Specifies the attribute by which to group the results.
parameters.name: dimension

schema.description: Specifies the attribute by which to group the results.

parameters.description: Array of names used to label the series in the response.
parameters.name: name

schema.description: Array of names used to label the series in the response.

parameters.description: Filters results by relative date range ending at the current time, with each value producing a separate series. Use `<n>d` for days (up to `364d`) or `<n>w` for weeks (up to `52w`). Append `control` to request the equivalent previous period for comparison: the comparison window is shifted back by the current window's length rounded up to a whole number of weeks, so it keeps the same weekday alignment and does not overlap the current window (e.g. `7dcontrol` covers days -14 to -7, `10dcontrol` covers days -24 to -14). For example, pass `7d` and `7dcontrol` to compare this week with the previous week. All series must resolve to the same duration as the main series; relative ranges (including `control`) satisfy this automatically. Use this parameter or set specific start and end dates (`dateStart` and `dateEnd` parameters).
parameters.name: dateRange

schema.description: Filters results by relative date range ending at the current time, with each value producing a separate series. Use `<n>d` for days (up to `364d`) or `<n>w` for weeks (up to `52w`). Append `control` to request the equivalent previous period for comparison: the comparison window is shifted back by the current window's length rounded up to a whole number of weeks, so it keeps the same weekday alignment and does not overlap the current window (e.g. `7dcontrol` covers days -14 to -7, `10dcontrol` covers days -24 to -14). For example, pass `7d` and `7dcontrol` to compare this week with the previous week. All series must resolve to the same duration as the main series; relative ranges (including `control`) satisfy this automatically. Use this parameter or set specific start and end dates (`dateStart` and `dateEnd` parameters).

parameters.description: Start of the date range. Alternative to `dateRange`; provide together with `dateEnd`. When requesting comparison series, every series must resolve to the same duration as the main series. Each `dateStart`/`dateEnd` is floored to the nearest 15 minutes before evaluation, so windows whose durations match only before alignment may be rejected.
parameters.name: dateStart

schema.description: Start of the date range. Alternative to `dateRange`; provide together with `dateEnd`. When requesting comparison series, every series must resolve to the same duration as the main series. Each `dateStart`/`dateEnd` is floored to the nearest 15 minutes before evaluation, so windows whose durations match only before alignment may be rejected.

parameters.description: End of the date range (inclusive). Alternative to `dateRange`; provide together with `dateStart`. When requesting comparison series, every series must resolve to the same duration as the main series. Each `dateStart`/`dateEnd` is floored to the nearest 15 minutes before evaluation, so windows whose durations match only before alignment may be rejected.
parameters.name: dateEnd

schema.description: End of the date range (inclusive). Alternative to `dateRange`; provide together with `dateStart`. When requesting comparison series, every series must resolve to the same duration as the main series. Each `dateStart`/`dateEnd` is floored to the nearest 15 minutes before evaluation, so windows whose durations match only before alignment may be rejected.

parameters.description: Limits the number of objects per group to the top items within the specified time range. When item count exceeds the limit, extra items appear grouped under an "other" category. Only supported on high-cardinality dimensions; otherwise the request is rejected. Minimum value is 2.
parameters.name: limitPerGroup

schema.description: Limits the number of objects per group to the top items within the specified time range. When item count exceeds the limit, extra items appear grouped under an "other" category. Only supported on high-cardinality dimensions; otherwise the request is rejected. Minimum value is 2.

parameters.description: Filters results by bot operator.
parameters.name: botOperator

schema.description: Filters results by bot operator.

parameters.description: Filters results by vertical.
parameters.name: vertical

schema.description: Filters results by vertical.

parameters.description: Filters results by industry.
parameters.name: industry

schema.description: Filters results by industry.

parameters.description: Filters results by agent type.
parameters.name: clientType

schema.description: Filters results by agent type.

parameters.description: Filters results by HTTP response status code (e.g. 200, 403, 404). Only [IANA-registered codes](https://www.iana.org/assignments/http-status-codes/http-status-codes.xhtml) are accepted.
parameters.name: responseStatus

schema.description: Filters results by HTTP response status code (e.g. 200, 403, 404). Only [IANA-registered codes](https://www.iana.org/assignments/http-status-codes/http-status-codes.xhtml) are accepted.

parameters.description: Filters results by HTTP response status code category.
parameters.name: responseStatusCategory

schema.description: Filters results by HTTP response status code category.

parameters.description: Format in which results will be returned.
parameters.name: format

schema.description: Format in which results will be returned.

items.description: Annotation associated with the result (e.g. outage or other type of event).

isInstantaneous.description: Whether event is a single point in time or a time range.

level.description: Provides an indication of how much confidence Cloudflare has in the data.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp of the last dataset update.

normalization.description: Normalization method applied to the results. Refer to [Normalization methods](https://developers.cloudflare.com/radar/concepts/normalization/).

additionalProperties.description: A numeric string that can include decimals and infinity values.

GET /radar/bots/crawlers/summary/{dimens.summary: Get crawler HTTP request distribution by dimension

GET /radar/bots/crawlers/timeseries_grou.description: Retrieves the distribution of HTTP requests from crawlers, grouped by the specified dimension over time.

parameters.description: Specifies the attribute by which to group the results.
parameters.name: dimension

schema.description: Specifies the attribute by which to group the results.

parameters.description: Aggregation interval of the results (e.g., in 15 minutes or 1 hour intervals). Refer to [Aggregation intervals](https://developers.cloudflare.com/radar/concepts/aggregation-intervals/). When omitted, the interval is auto-selected from the requested date range; finer intervals are only available for shorter ranges. If the requested interval is too granular for the date range, the request is rejected.
parameters.name: aggInterval

schema.description: Aggregation interval of the results (e.g., in 15 minutes or 1 hour intervals). Refer to [Aggregation intervals](https://developers.cloudflare.com/radar/concepts/aggregation-intervals/). When omitted, the interval is auto-selected from the requested date range; finer intervals are only available for shorter ranges. If the requested interval is too granular for the date range, the request is rejected.

parameters.name: name

parameters.description: Normalization method applied to the results. Refer to [Normalization methods](https://developers.cloudflare.com/radar/concepts/normalization/). `PERCENTAGE_CHANGE` requires exactly one comparison series (e.g. a `control` date range).
